source.author: SSA Catalog
source.notes: Classic beef shawarma marinade: warm spices + garlic + citrus, balanced for roast, grill, or pan.

ingredients.label: Red wine vinegar
notes: Sub: apple cider vinegar or white vinegar.

ingredients.label: Onion, finely grated (or very finely minced)
notes: About 1 small onion. Grating creates juice that tenderizes and flavors the beef.
If you hate onion texture, strain the pulp and use just the juice.

ingredients.label: Tomato paste
notes: About 1 tablespoon; optional but adds depth and color.

ingredients.label: Smoked paprika
notes: About 1 teaspoon; optional for smoky depth.

ingredients.label: Ground cardamom
notes: About 1/4 teaspoon; optional but very authentic.

ingredients.label: Black pepper, freshly ground
notes: About 3/4 teaspoon; increase if you like pepper bite.

ingredients.label: Kosher salt
notes: About 1 3/4 teaspoons. This is a marinade for ~1.3–1.8 kg (3–4 lb) beef.
If you will salt again before cooking, reduce to 7 g.

ingredients.label: Brown sugar (or honey)
notes: Optional: helps browning and balances acidity (about 2 teaspoons).

steps.title: Build the marinade
instructions: In a bowl, whisk together olive oil, lemon juice, vinegar, grated garlic, grated onion, and optional tomato paste until smooth.
Whisk in all spices (cumin, coriander, paprika, smoked paprika, allspice, cinnamon, cloves, optional cardamom), black pepper, salt, optional sugar/honey, and optional chili flakes.

steps.title: Marinate the beef
instructions: Add beef (sliced steak, strips, or thin-cut roast pieces) to a zip bag or covered container.
Pour marinade over beef and massage to coat evenly.
Refrigerate: minimum 2 hours; ideal 8–24 hours for best flavor.

steps.title: Cooking guidance (notes)
instructions: For best shawarma texture, slice beef thinly across the grain.
High-heat methods work best: hot skillet, grill, or hot oven/roast.
Pat excess marinade off the surface if it’s very wet to improve browning.

steps.title: Food safety
instructions: Marinate in the refrigerator (not on the counter).
Discard used marinade that touched raw meat, or bring it to a full boil before using as a sauce.
